title: Recall access tracking stays local and ignored
summary: Kage recall now records memory reuse in .agent memory/reports/memory access.json instead of mutating shareable packet JSON. The report tracks total uses, 30 day uses, best/last rank, hot/cold packet counts, and feeds a s
body:
Kage recall now records memory reuse in .agent_memory/reports/memory-access.json instead of mutating shareable packet JSON. The report tracks total uses, 30-day uses, best/last rank, hot/cold packet counts, and feeds a small bounded usage score into future recall explanations and viewer dashboard cards.
Evidence: Implemented in mcp/kernel.ts, mcp/cli.ts, mcp/index.ts, mcp/daemon.ts, mcp/viewer/app.js, docs/viewer/app.js, README.md, mcp/README.md, and docs/guide.html.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; node --check mcp/viewer/app.js; node --check docs/viewer/app.js
